seo.title: Honduras Market Intelligence | Perfect Mission
seo.description: Honduras has selective tourism and coastal upside, but security and property restrictions make it a high-friction market for foreign-led development.

hero.title: Honduras: selective upside, heavy friction
hero.subtitle: Potential exists in controlled tourism zones — but this is not a market to approach casually.

summary.one_line_verdict: Honduras may offer pockets of coastal upside, but the combination of security risk and property restrictions makes it a poor default platform market.

market_stage.label: Early / high-friction
market_stage.description: The market is early enough to look tempting, especially on islands and coasts, but that earlyness comes wrapped in legal and operational constraints.

summary.operator_angle: Works only with strong local control, clear tourism-zone structuring, and strict safety discipline.
summary.investor_angle: A hard sell to conservative capital unless the opportunity is unusually de-risked and small.
summary.base_case: Keep Honduras as a highly selective, tourism-zone watch market rather than a current priority deployment geography.

quick_facts.label: Headline corporate tax
quick_facts.value: 25% on net taxable income, with additional surcharge mechanics in some cases

quick_facts.label: Foreign property rule
quick_facts.value: Foreigners can buy property near shorelines in designated tourism zones, subject to specific limits and approvals

quick_facts.label: Safety signal
quick_facts.value: U.S. State Department maintains a Level 3 ‘Reconsider Travel’ advisory, with some areas listed as ‘Do not travel’

quick_facts.label: Tourism signal
quick_facts.value: UN Tourism reports 2.8 million international visitors in 2024, up 17.8% YoY

strategy_fit.why: Possible only where legal structure and security are fully understood.
Restrictions and local complexity make this a poor default approach.
Too much operating friction for a default build-out market.

development_reality.headline: Possible, but not casual
development_reality.copy: Honduras is the kind of market where an operator can talk themselves into upside because the map looks cheap. The governing reality is that coastal and tourism-zone rules, safety, and local execution quality dominate the outcome.
avoid_first: assuming islands are straightforward because foreigners are present
process_notes: Legal access changes by zone and property type.
Security is part of underwriting, not a side issue.
This is not a broad-market rollout candidate.

foreign_ownership.summary: Foreign property rights exist, but important geographic restrictions apply, especially near coasts and borders.
foreign_ownership.caution: Any coastal or island project should be treated as a specialised legal exercise, not a routine transaction.

priority_cities.thesis: The obvious area to investigate, but also the easiest place to underestimate legal and security nuance.
Interesting only with local execution and hard legal structuring.
Better for local-economy understanding than for an international lifestyle pitch.

catalysts: There are specific tourism-zone opportunities that can attract foreign attention.
The country remains less crowded by international real-estate capital than many peers.
risks: Foreign ownership constraints and tourism-zone rules complicate underwriting.
Weak local execution can destroy value quickly.
This is not a low-friction market for remote operators.

lifestyle_base.copy: Possible for a niche operator with deep local ties; weak for a default international base.
lifestyle_base.home_office_fit: Not the main question; operating risk dominates.

cta.headline: Need the verified version?
cta.body: Request a country memo or submit a specific site, listing, plot, or market thesis for verification.
